commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=5db330bda4e3d68be1b97c79220dfef8d6a90254)
commands.review_summary:
The PR removes a second merge inside `FeishuStreamingSession.update()` and suppresses `onPartialReply` updates after Feishu block delivery takes over a streaming card.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main treats CardKit content updates as suffix appends, while reasoning/status streaming can produce non-prefix rewritten snapshots that this PR still appends. I did not run a live Feishu tenant session.
commands.review_followup: Needs human follow-up because the external PR lacks real behavior proof and the remaining append-versus-replace delivery behavior must be fixed and validated before merge.

target.title: fix(feishu): eliminate streaming card content duplication
target.body:
## Problem

Feishu streaming card messages show duplicated content — earlier text reappears concatenated with newer text within the same card.

## Root Cause

`FeishuStreamingSession.update()` calls `mergeStreamingText(currentText, nextText)` **a second time** inside the async queue callback (after a first call already produced `mergedInput` upstream). When reasoning updates cause `buildCombinedStreamText` to produce content that does not extend cleanly from `currentText`, `mergeStreamingText` falls back to plain concatenation (`prev + next`), sending `currentText + nextText` to `updateCardContent` — duplicating whatever was already on the card.

`updateCardContent` issues a **full PUT replace** on the CardKit card, so the in-queue re-merge is both redundant and harmful.

## Fix

**`streaming-card.ts`** — remove the redundant `mergeStreamingText` call in `update()`:
- Use `nextText` directly; it already holds the authoritative latest content
- Update `appendContent`, `currentText`, and `sentText` consistently against `nextText`

**`reply-dispatcher.ts`** — add `suppressOnPartialReply` flag:
- In `message_tool_only` mode (group-chat room events, introduced in 5.16), both `onPartialReply` and block-delivery `deliver()` fire with overlapping content
- Once block delivery takes ownership of the streaming card, `onPartialReply` snapshot updates must stop to prevent them from racing with block-delta updates
- Flag is reset at the start of each reply (`onReplyStart`) so per-reply isolation is preserved

## Testing

Verified locally against `@openclaw/feishu` v2026.5.12 (community plugin) with a group-chat room event trigger using an extended-thinking model. Before the fix: card content repeated on every reasoning token flush. After the fix: card updates cleanly without duplication.
target.author: hpumajun
